outcome_text: Responses to the AI risks deemed high priority, as identified by the MAP and MEASURE functions, are developed, planned, and documented. Risk response options can include mitigating, transferring, avoiding, or accepting.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI risk response strategy development, including designing mitigation measures, evaluating risk transfer options, defining risk avoidance criteria, establishing risk acceptance thresholds, and documenting planned responses for high-priority AI risks identified through MAP and MEASURE activities.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI risk response strategy development, including designing mitigation measures, evaluating risk transfer options, defining risk avoidance criteria, establishing risk acceptance thresholds, and documenting planned responses for high-priority AI risks identified through MAP and MEASURE activities.
reference_document_element_description: Conduct AI system risk classification under the EU AI Act, documenting risk category determination, applicable requirements identification, and conformity assessment pathway selection.
comments: NIST AI RMF MG-1.3 (MANAGE) superset of WIDAI RC domain. RC-T-012 is the strongest match (STS: 0.599, cosine: 0.727). Secondary: AG-S-005 (AG, cosine: 0.725), AG-K-010 (AG, cosine: 0.715).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.0/10 (raw 0.599). Shared vocabulary: [ai, documenting, risk]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [acceptance, activities, avoidance, criteria, defining, designing] vs WIDAI terms [act, applicable, assessment, category, classification, conduct] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.880 (P=0.872, R=0.889). Balanced P/R — the 37-word competency implication and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (98.2%). Semantic tension between "Knowledge of AI risk response strategy development, including designing mitigati" and "Conduct AI system risk classification under the EU AI Act, documenting risk cate". Despite STS-based 'Superset of' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.727 > STS 0.599 (gap +0.128). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, documenting, risk] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.2%. Shared tokens: [ai, documenting, risk]. NIST AI RMF-only: [acceptance, activities, avoidance, criteria, defining, designing]. WIDAI-only: [act, applicable, assessment, category, classification, conduct].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 98.2% on Superset of classification. The matched KSA operates in a different professional domain than the obligation/competency requires. STS 0.599 passed the threshold but secondary signals contradict the match.
Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.128 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
